skill.name: ceo-advisor
skill.description: Executive leadership guidance for strategic planning, board governance, investor relations, financial scenario analysis, organizational culture, and CEO decision workflows.
skill.summary: Guide CEO strategy, governance, investor communication, and leadership decisions.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: AI review found that the static external-command, weak-cryptography, reconnaissance, and certificate findings are false positives from Markdown fences and business terminology. The Python scripts use standard library calculations and example data, with no evidence of network access, subprocess execution, environment access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration. The skill has low residual risk because it includes local helper scripts and documented commands to run them.

low_findings.title: Local Helper Scripts Require Normal User Review
low_findings.description: The skill includes two Python helper scripts for strategic and financial analysis. They import only standard library modules and operate on provided example business data, so this is a low residual risk rather than a confirmed security issue.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script files are real executable helpers, but inspected imports show no subprocess, network, environment, or file write capability. The risk is limited to running local analysis code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Are Controlled
low_findings.description: Static shell-execution findings in SKILL.md correspond to fenced examples that run bundled Python scripts. The commands are fixed strings and do not interpolate user input, so no command-injection path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported lines are Markdown examples, not dynamic code execution. The shown commands are static local invocations of repository scripts.

low_findings.title: Reference Documents Misread As Executable Patterns
low_findings.description: The external-command reports in reference Markdown files are templates, diagrams, and business documents. They are not executable Ruby or shell code and contain no instruction to run arbitrary commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown code fences containing email templates, decision trees, and team charters. They have no executable interpreter context.

low_findings.title: Business Terms Misread As Cryptography Or Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static high and low findings for weak cryptography, certificate keys, and reconnaissance map to ordinary business words such as key metrics, market analysis, and governance terms. No cryptographic API, certificate material, host discovery, or system reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported lines contain business framework language and list labels, not security-sensitive APIs or secrets. Supporting script inspection did not reveal crypto, certificate, or reconnaissance code.

content.value_statement: Executive decisions need structured analysis across strategy, finance, governance, and culture. This skill provides CEO-ready frameworks, templates, and local analysis helpers for high-stakes planning.
actual_capabilities: Structures CEO decisions with strategic, financial, risk, and stakeholder criteria.
Provides board meeting, board package, investor update, and earnings communication templates.
Models financial scenarios with base cases, probabilities, projections, NPV, and risk-adjusted recommendations.
Assesses strategic position using market, financial, operational, organizational, and growth pillars.
Guides culture transformation, executive team design, succession planning, and crisis leadership workflows.
limitations: Does not replace legal, financial, tax, or securities counsel.
Uses user-provided assumptions and example data, so outputs require executive validation.
Does not connect to live market, accounting, cap table, or investor relations systems.
The helper scripts are local examples and may need adaptation for company-specific models.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Board Meeting
use_cases.description: Create a focused agenda, CEO letter outline, dashboard themes, and decision requests for directors.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Strategic Options
use_cases.description: Compare expansion, acquisition, turnaround, or digital transformation options against risk and execution constraints.

use_cases.title: Plan Culture Transformation
use_cases.description: Map leadership behaviors, operating rhythms, talent systems, and communication steps for a culture change program.

prompt_templates.title: Create a CEO Decision Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CEO decision brief for [decision]. Include context, options, tradeoffs, risks, stakeholders, recommendation, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Board Materials
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a board package outline for [meeting goal]. Include agenda, CEO narrative, dashboard metrics, financial review, strategic updates, risks, and requested decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Strategic Scenarios
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these strategic scenarios: [scenario list]. Compare investment, timing, upside, downside, execution risk, stakeholder impact, and recommended sequencing.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Executive Operating System
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an executive operating system for [company stage]. Include leadership cadence, decision rights, scorecards, culture mechanisms, board communication, and crisis escalation paths.

output_examples.input: We need to decide whether to enter a new geographic market next year.
output_examples.output: A CEO-ready decision brief with market rationale, strategic options, required investment, risk register, stakeholder map, decision criteria, and a recommended go or no-go path.

output_examples.input: Prepare the next board update after a missed revenue quarter.
output_examples.output: A board communication outline that leads with facts, explains root causes, shows mitigation actions, clarifies cash impact, and asks for specific director guidance.

output_examples.input: Our leadership team needs a stronger culture and accountability model.
output_examples.output: A culture transformation plan with target behaviors, executive team charter, operating cadence, talent review structure, communication plan, and progress metrics.

best_practices: Provide current company metrics, constraints, time horizon, and stakeholder context before asking for recommendations.
Ask for assumptions, risks, and decision criteria to be separated from recommendations.
Review outputs with qualified legal, finance, and governance advisors before external use.
anti_patterns: Do not use generated board, investor, or earnings content without human review.
Do not treat scenario outputs as forecasts when assumptions are incomplete or untested.
Do not ask for confidential material to be included unless your environment is approved for that data.

faq.question: What does this skill help CEOs do?
faq.answer: It helps structure strategy, governance, investor communication, financial scenarios, culture work, and executive decisions.

faq.question: Can it create board materials?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes board agenda, CEO update, dashboard, risk, and governance templates.

faq.question: Does it provide financial advice?
faq.answer: No. It supports scenario analysis, but qualified finance, tax, and legal advisors should review decisions.

faq.question: Does it access live company systems?
faq.answer: No. It relies on user-provided information and local helper scripts.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide company stage, goals, metrics, constraints, timeline, stakeholders, and decisions required.
